The 1.0% coating of polysorbate-80 on the surface of many NPs [32–35]. Polysorb-
ate-80 acclimates surface action by the adsorption of apolipoproteins and mimics as
Low-density lipoproteins (LDL) to cross blood brain barrier via receptor-mediated
endocytosis,  inhibiting  P-glycoprotein  efflux  [36,  37].  Afterward,  P-80  was  coated
on  Neurotoxin-NPs  which  was  administered  via  intranasal  route  resulted  enhance-
ment of bioavailability of Neurotoxin in brain as compared to Neurotoxin-Solution
and  delivered  the  Neurotoxin-NPs  (i.v.  route)  [38,  39].  Though,  this  is  a  fact  that
enhancement of drug in the brain can be possible by the use coating of P-80 NPs
administered by the different routes i.n.; oral, intraperitoneal, i.v. This is very good
indication that the use of Polysorbate-80 cab be targeted the brain easily. Addition-
ally, in the field of pharmaceutical formulation, P-80 is an excellent absorption and
permeation enhancer of drug. The P-80 desorption was coated on the surface of NPs
can also give the enhancing effect of absorption.

column2: There are so many methods reported before for MNG but most of them are based

column1: on herbal analysis [40–43]. Some of them were also reported various methods for
the  quantification  of  MNG  in  the  plasma  only  [44–46].  There  was  one  method
reported  which  is  based  on  simultaneous  method  with  other  constituents  [47].
Finally, no bioanalytical method has been available for MNG in the plasma as well
as brain homogenate samples. This is the most important reason to develop a bioan-
alytical method for the quantification of MNG in the plasma as well as brain tissues.
It is also important to develop a very sensitive bioanalytical method to examine the
MNG upto picogram level. Based on, above literature support and the observation, a
novel MNG bioanalytical method developed which is a first time for the determina-
tion of MNG in brain as well as plasma. This bioanalytical LC–MS/MS method was
used to determine the concentration of MNG for P 80-coated PLGA NPs treatment
and also validated successfully. The bioanalytical method have developed success-
fully upto picogram level with highly efficient, more sensitive, very small retention
time in plasma as well as brain pharmacokinetics.
